'''Michael Jackson''' (born 11 October 1969) is a former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1980s and 1990s. He played at representative level for [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]], and at club level for [[Hunslet R.L.F.C.|Hunslet]], [[Wakefield Trinity]], [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]] and [[Sheffield Eagles]], as a {{rlp|sr}} or {{rlp|lf}}.

==Career==
Jackson started his professional career with [[Hunslet R.L.F.C.|Hunslet]] before being signed by [[Wakefield Trinity]] in 1991 for a fee of £60,000.<ref>{{cite news |last1=Huxley |first1=John |title=Trinity's second row pass the Test |url=https://www.proquest.com/docview/187216476 |work=The Guardian |date=16 November 1991 |page=20 |id={{ProQuest|187216476}} |url-access=subscription }}</ref> In July 1993, Jackson signed a two-year contract with [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Rugby League: Widnes place Eyres on the transfer list |url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/rugby-league-widnes-place-eyres-on-the-transfer-list-1485267.html |website=The Independent |access-date=17 August 2023 |date=15 July 1993}}</ref>

Jackson was signed by [[Sheffield Eagles]] in January 1998 for a fee of £28,000.<ref>{{cite web |title=Rugby League: Jackson bargain for Eagles |url=https://www.independent.co.uk/news/education/education-news/rugby-league-jackson-bargain-for-eagles-1140544.html |website=The Independent |access-date=17 August 2023 |date=24 January 1998}}</ref> He appeared as a [[Substitution (sport)|substitute]] in Sheffield Eagles' 17–8 victory over [[Wigan Warriors]] in the [[1998 Challenge Cup]] Final during [[Super League III]] at [[Wembley Stadium (1923)|Wembley Stadium]], [[London]] on Saturday 2 May 1998. Following the club's merger with [[Huddersfield Giants]] in 1999, he joined the newly-formed Sheffield Eagles club ahead of the [[2000 Northern Ford Premiership]] season.<ref>{{cite web |last1=Hadfield |first1=Dave |title=Rugby League: Rebirth of the Eagles fulfils an ambition for Aston |url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/rugby-league-rebirth-of-the-eagles-fulfils-an-ambition-for-aston-1131109.html |website=The Independent |access-date=1 July 2022 |date=8 December 1999}}</ref>

===International honours===
Jackson won [[Cap (sport)|cap]]s for [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] while at Wakefield Trinity in 1991 against Papua New Guinea, in 1992 against France, Australia (sub), and New Zealand (sub), and while at [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]] in 1993 against New Zealand (2 matches) (sub).<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org">{{cite web|url=http://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/players/michael-jackson/summary.html|title=Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org|publisher=rugbyleagueproject.org|date=31 December 2011|accessdate=1 January 2012}}</ref> He was selected to go on the [[1992 Great Britain Lions tour of Australia and New Zealand]].
